name: Pop Culture
description: From binge-worthy TV to viral memes — master the shows, games, heroes, and moments that define modern entertainment culture.

facts.quizQuestion: I Love Lucy, the groundbreaking 1951 sitcom starring Lucille Ball, originally aired on which network?
facts.explanation: I Love Lucy premiered on CBS in October 1951 and ran for 6 seasons. Co-created by Lucille Ball and Desi Arnaz, it became one of the most influential sitcoms in TV history and was the first show with a live studio audience recorded on film. Source: Wikipedia/I_Love_Lucy

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million Americans watched the I Love Lucy episode 'Lucy Goes to the Hospital' in 1953?
facts.explanation: The 'Lucy Goes to the Hospital' episode drew approximately 44 million viewers when it aired in January 1953 — more people than watched President Eisenhower's inauguration the very next day. It remains one of the most-watched broadcasts in TV history. Source: Wikipedia/I_Love_Lucy

facts.quizQuestion: Who co-created the NBC sitcom Seinfeld alongside Larry David?
facts.explanation: Jerry Seinfeld and Larry David co-created Seinfeld, which ran for 9 seasons on NBC (1989-1998). The show about 'nothing' became one of the most influential sitcoms in TV history, and its 1998 finale was watched by 76.3 million viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Seinfeld

facts.quizQuestion: How many million viewers tuned in to watch the Seinfeld series finale in 1998?
facts.explanation: The Seinfeld finale in May 1998 was watched by approximately 76.3 million viewers, making it one of the most-watched TV events in history. Advertisers paid $1 million per 30 seconds of commercial time during the finale. Source: Wikipedia/Seinfeld

facts.quizQuestion: Marta Kauffman co-created Friends with which other television writer?
facts.explanation: Friends was created by David Crane and Marta Kauffman, premiering on NBC in 1994. The show ran for 10 seasons and its 2004 finale drew 52.5 million viewers, making it the most-watched entertainment broadcast of the 2000s decade. Source: Wikipedia/Friends

facts.quizQuestion: How many seasons did Friends run on NBC?
facts.explanation: Friends ran for 10 seasons on NBC from 1994 to 2004. The show about a group of friends living in New York City became one of the most popular sitcoms in television history, with the finale drawing 52.5 million viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Friends

facts.quizQuestion: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air, which launched Will Smith's acting career, originally aired on which network starting in 1990?
facts.explanation: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air premiered on NBC in September 1990 and ran for 6 seasons. Created by Andy and Susan Borowitz, the show launched Will Smith from rapper to international acting superstar. Source: Wikipedia/The_Fresh_Prince_of_Bel-Air

facts.quizQuestion: How many Emmy Awards did Cheers win across its 11-season run on NBC, a record for a comedy series at the time?
facts.explanation: Cheers won 28 Emmy Awards across its 11 seasons on NBC (1982-1993), including Outstanding Comedy Series four times. Created by Glen Charles, Les Charles, and James Burrows, the show's 1993 finale drew 93 million viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Cheers

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million viewers watched the Cheers series finale in 1993?
facts.explanation: The Cheers finale in May 1993 attracted approximately 93 million viewers, making it one of the most-watched broadcasts in American TV history. The show ran 11 seasons on NBC and won 28 Emmy Awards. Source: Wikipedia/Cheers

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million viewers watched the M*A*S*H series finale in 1983, making it the most-watched broadcast in TV history until 2010?
facts.explanation: The M*A*S*H finale 'Goodbye, Farewell and Amen' in February 1983 drew approximately 125 million viewers — the most-watched broadcast in American TV history until the 2010 Super Bowl. The show ran 11 seasons on CBS. Source: Wikipedia/M*A*S*H_(TV_series)

facts.quizQuestion: Which cartoonist created The Simpsons, the longest-running animated series in American TV history?
facts.explanation: Matt Groening created The Simpsons, which premiered on Fox in December 1989. With 37+ seasons and counting, it is the longest-running American animated series, and Homer's catchphrase 'D'oh!' was added to the Oxford English Dictionary in 2001. Source: Wikipedia/The_Simpsons

facts.quizQuestion: The Simpsons has aired on which network since its 1989 premiere?
facts.explanation: The Simpsons has aired on Fox since December 1989 and with 37+ seasons it is the longest-running American animated series and the longest-running American scripted primetime television series. Source: Wikipedia/The_Simpsons

facts.quizQuestion: Which showrunner created The Sopranos, the HBO drama credited with launching the 'Second Golden Age of Television'?
facts.explanation: David Chase created The Sopranos, which ran on HBO from 1999 to 2007 for 6 seasons. The show won 21 Emmy Awards and is widely credited with ushering in the 'Second Golden Age of Television' with its cinematic storytelling about a New Jersey mob boss. Source: Wikipedia/The_Sopranos

facts.quizQuestion: Which writer created Breaking Bad, which holds a Guinness World Record as the most critically acclaimed TV show ever?
facts.explanation: Vince Gilligan created Breaking Bad, which aired on AMC from 2008 to 2013 for 5 seasons. The show earned 16 Emmy Awards and holds the Guinness World Record for the highest-rated TV series, with a Metacritic score of 99/100. Source: Wikipedia/Breaking_Bad

facts.quizQuestion: Breaking Bad, creator Vince Gilligan's story of a chemistry teacher turned drug manufacturer, aired on which cable network?
facts.explanation: Breaking Bad aired on AMC from 2008 to 2013. AMC, originally a classic movies channel, transformed into a prestige drama powerhouse with Breaking Bad and Mad Men airing in the same era, winning a combined 26+ Emmy Awards. Source: Wikipedia/Breaking_Bad

facts.quizQuestion: Which former Baltimore Sun journalist created The Wire, the HBO drama now widely considered the greatest TV show ever made?
facts.explanation: David Simon, a former Baltimore Sun crime reporter, created The Wire, which aired on HBO from 2002 to 2008 for 5 seasons. Despite low initial ratings, it is now frequently ranked as the greatest TV drama ever made for its unflinching portrayal of Baltimore institutions. Source: Wikipedia/The_Wire

facts.quizQuestion: How many consecutive years did Mad Men win the Outstanding Drama Emmy Award, from 2008 to 2011?
facts.explanation: Mad Men, created by Matthew Weiner, won the Outstanding Drama Series Emmy Award four consecutive years from 2008 to 2011 — an unprecedented achievement at the time. The AMC series ran for 7 seasons from 2007 to 2015. Source: Wikipedia/Mad_Men

facts.quizQuestion: How many Emmy Awards did Game of Thrones win in total, more than any other drama series in history?
facts.explanation: Game of Thrones won 59 Emmy Awards across its 8-season run (2011-2019) on HBO — the most Emmy wins ever for a drama series. Created by David Benioff and D.B. Weiss, the final season averaged 11.99 million viewers per episode. Source: Wikipedia/Game_of_Thrones

facts.quizQuestion: Matt Stone co-created South Park on Comedy Central with which other writer and animator?
facts.explanation: Trey Parker and Matt Stone created South Park, which premiered on Comedy Central in August 1997 and has run for 28+ seasons. The show is famous for producing episodes within approximately one week of their air date, often satirizing current events. Source: Wikipedia/South_Park

facts.quizQuestion: Which voice actor and writer created Family Guy, which famously premiered after Super Bowl XXXIII in 1999?
facts.explanation: Seth MacFarlane created Family Guy, which premiered on Fox in January 1999 after Super Bowl XXXIII. The show was canceled twice before becoming a hit through DVD sales and Adult Swim reruns, eventually running for 24+ seasons. Source: Wikipedia/Family_Guy

facts.quizQuestion: Rick and Morty, created by Justin Roiland and Dan Harmon, originally premiered on which late-night animation block in 2013?
facts.explanation: Rick and Morty premiered on Adult Swim (Cartoon Network's late-night block) in December 2013. The show's cultural impact was demonstrated when McDonald's brought back Szechuan Sauce in 2017 after fans cited a Rick and Morty episode as the reason it should return. Source: Wikipedia/Rick_and_Morty

facts.quizQuestion: Avatar: The Last Airbender, created by DiMartino and Konietzko, originally aired on which children's network starting in 2005?
facts.explanation: Avatar: The Last Airbender aired on Nickelodeon from 2005 to 2008 for 3 seasons. Decades after its original run, the show debuted on Netflix and immediately reached #1, demonstrating its enduring popularity with multiple generations of viewers. Source: Wikipedia/Avatar:_The_Last_Airbender

facts.quizQuestion: Which marine science educator turned animator created SpongeBob SquarePants for Nickelodeon in 1999?
facts.explanation: Stephen Hillenburg, a marine science educator who later studied animation, created SpongeBob SquarePants, which premiered on Nickelodeon in 1999. The franchise has generated over $16 billion in merchandise revenue, making it one of the most profitable animated properties in history. Source: Wikipedia/SpongeBob_SquarePants

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million viewers watched the Survivor: The Australian Outback premiere in 2001, a record for the series?
facts.explanation: The Survivor: The Australian Outback premiere drew approximately 45.37 million viewers in January 2001, the highest-rated episode in the show's history. Survivor, created by Charlie Parsons, premiered on CBS in 2000 and has run for 50+ seasons. Source: Wikipedia/Survivor_(American_TV_series)

facts.quizQuestion: How many consecutive seasons was American Idol the #1 show in America, from 2004?
facts.explanation: American Idol was the #1 rated show in America for 7 consecutive seasons starting from 2004. Created by Simon Fuller, the show premiered on Fox in 2002 and later moved to ABC after a hiatus. Source: Wikipedia/American_Idol

facts.quizQuestion: How many consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Host did RuPaul win for hosting RuPaul's Drag Race?
facts.explanation: RuPaul won 8 consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Host for a Reality or Reality-Competition Program for RuPaul's Drag Race, which premiered on Logo TV in 2009 before moving to VH1. The show is produced by World of Wonder. Source: Wikipedia/RuPaul's_Drag_Race

facts.quizQuestion: Stranger Things, created by the Duffer Brothers and set in 1980s Indiana, is an original series exclusive to which streaming platform?
facts.explanation: Stranger Things premiered on Netflix in July 2016, created by Matt and Ross Duffer (the Duffer Brothers). The show's nostalgic 1980s setting and supernatural storylines made it a global hit, and in Season 4 it caused Kate Bush's 1985 song 'Running Up That Hill' to surge back to #1 on music charts worldwide. Source: Wikipedia/Stranger_Things

facts.quizQuestion: Which South Korean filmmaker created Squid Game, Netflix's most-watched series of all time?
facts.explanation: Hwang Dong-hyuk created Squid Game, which premiered on Netflix in September 2021 and became the platform's most-watched series ever. The show won 6 Emmy Awards, including the first Emmy for a non-English-language drama series. Source: Wikipedia/Squid_Game

facts.quizQuestion: In its first week on Netflix in November 2022, approximately how many million hours was the Wednesday TV series streamed?
facts.explanation: Wednesday, created by Alfred Gough and Miles Millar and directed by Tim Burton, was streamed for 341 million hours in its first week on Netflix in November 2022 — a record at the time. The show reached #1 in 91 countries. Source: Wikipedia/Wednesday_(TV_series)

facts.quizQuestion: The Mandalorian, created by Jon Favreau and featuring 'Baby Yoda', is a streaming original exclusive to which platform?
facts.explanation: The Mandalorian premiered on Disney+ in November 2019 as a launch title for the new streaming service. Created by Jon Favreau, it uses revolutionary StageCraft LED volume technology and introduced 'Baby Yoda' (Grogu), who became a massive cultural phenomenon. Source: Wikipedia/The_Mandalorian

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million viewers watched Tiger King on Netflix within the first 10 days of its release in March 2020?
facts.explanation: Tiger King drew approximately 34.3 million viewers within its first 10 days on Netflix in March 2020, during the early COVID-19 lockdowns. The documentary about exotic animal owner Joe Exotic became a viral cultural phenomenon almost overnight. Source: Wikipedia/Tiger_King

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Lost premiere on ABC, winning the Emmy for Outstanding Drama in 2005?
facts.explanation: Lost premiered on ABC in September 2004, created by J.J. Abrams, Jeffrey Lieber, and Damon Lindelof. The mystery drama about plane crash survivors on a mysterious island ran for 6 seasons and won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series in 2005. Source: Wikipedia/Lost_(TV_series)

facts.quizQuestion: Which American writer adapted the British version of The Office for NBC, where it ran for 9 seasons beginning in 2005?
facts.explanation: Greg Daniels adapted the British Office (created by Ricky Gervais and Stephen Merchant) for American audiences on NBC, where it ran from 2005 to 2013 for 9 seasons. The American version won the Emmy for Outstanding Comedy Series in 2006. Source: Wikipedia/The_Office_(American_TV_series)

facts.quizQuestion: Greg Daniels co-created Parks and Recreation with which other writer, who later created The Good Place?
facts.explanation: Parks and Recreation was co-created by Greg Daniels and Michael Schur, premiering on NBC in 2009 and running for 7 seasons. Time magazine named it the #1 TV show of 2012. Michael Schur later created Brooklyn Nine-Nine and The Good Place. Source: Wikipedia/Parks_and_Recreation

facts.quizQuestion: Which writer created Arrested Development, the cult-classic comedy that aired on Fox, was canceled, then revived on Netflix?
facts.explanation: Mitchell Hurwitz created Arrested Development, which premiered on Fox in 2003 and ran for 3 seasons before cancellation, then was revived by Netflix for Season 4 (2013) and Season 5 (2018-2019). Despite low ratings during its original run, it won 5 Emmy Awards including Outstanding Comedy Series. Source: Wikipedia/Arrested_Development_(TV_series)

facts.quizQuestion: Which Namco designer created the 1980 arcade classic Pac-Man?
facts.explanation: Toru Iwatani designed Pac-Man, released by Namco in 1980. He reportedly was inspired by a pizza with a missing slice. The game went on to generate $12 billion in cumulative revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pac-Man

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many billion dollars in cumulative revenue has Pac-Man generated since its 1980 release?
facts.explanation: Pac-Man has generated approximately $12 billion in cumulative revenue across arcade machines, home ports, merchandise, and spin-offs. The original arcade game sold about 43 million units. Source: Wikipedia/Pac-Man

facts.quizQuestion: Which Soviet software engineer created Tetris in 1984?
facts.explanation: Alexey Pajitnov created Tetris in 1984 while working at the Soviet Academy of Sciences. He initially received no royalties; the game became a commercial sensation in 1988 and has sold over 520 million copies across all versions. Source: Wikipedia/Tetris

facts.quizQuestion: How many million copies of Tetris have been sold across all versions, making it the second-best-selling game series?
facts.explanation: Tetris has sold over 520 million copies across all platforms and versions, making it the second-best-selling video game series ever. It was first released commercially in 1988 and remains widely played today. Source: Wikipedia/Tetris

facts.quizQuestion: Which Nintendo designer created Super Mario Bros. in 1985?
facts.explanation: Shigeru Miyamoto designed Super Mario Bros., released for the NES in 1985. The game sold over 58 million copies and a 1990 study found Mario was more recognizable to American children than Mickey Mouse. Source: Wikipedia/Super_Mario_Bros.

facts.quizQuestion: A 1990 study found that Mario was more recognizable to American children than which iconic Disney character?
facts.explanation: A 1990 survey found that Mario was more recognizable among American children than Mickey Mouse, illustrating Nintendo's cultural dominance during that era. Super Mario Bros. had sold over 58 million copies by that point. Source: Wikipedia/Super_Mario_Bros.

facts.quizQuestion: The Legend of Zelda (1986) was the first NES cartridge to feature what technology that let players save their progress?
facts.explanation: The Legend of Zelda (1986) was the first NES cartridge to use battery-backed saving, allowing players to save their progress between sessions. Previously, players had to use passwords or start over. It sold 6.51 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/The_Legend_of_Zelda_(video_game)

facts.quizQuestion: Sonic the Hedgehog helped which Sega console outsell the Super Nintendo 2:1 in 1991-1992?
facts.explanation: Sonic the Hedgehog (1991) was so popular it helped the Sega Genesis outsell the Super Nintendo 2:1 during 1991-1992, a remarkable feat in the console wars era. Sonic sold over 40 million copies across the series. Source: Wikipedia/Sonic_the_Hedgehog_(video_game)

facts.quizQuestion: In the 1981 arcade game Donkey Kong, Mario made his debut under what original name?
facts.explanation: Mario first appeared in the 1981 Donkey Kong arcade game under the name 'Jumpman.' The game was designed by Shigeru Miyamoto and generated $180 million by June 1982. Jumpman was later renamed Mario in later games. Source: Wikipedia/Donkey_Kong_(video_game)

facts.quizQuestion: The original PlayStation became the first console to exceed how many million units sold?
facts.explanation: The original PlayStation, released in Japan in 1994, sold 102.49 million units — making it the first console to exceed 100 million units sold. It was designed under Ken Kutaragi, who became known as 'the father of PlayStation.' Source: Wikipedia/PlayStation_(console)

facts.quizQuestion: Which Sony engineer, often called 'the father of PlayStation,' led the design of the original PlayStation?
facts.explanation: Ken Kutaragi was the primary architect of the PlayStation gaming platform and is widely known as 'the father of PlayStation.' The original PlayStation (1994) sold 102.49 million units and was the first console to surpass 100 million units sold. Source: Wikipedia/PlayStation_(console)

facts.quizQuestion: Which Sony console, with 160 million units sold, remains the best-selling video game console of all time?
facts.explanation: The PlayStation 2, released in 2000, sold over 160 million units and remains the best-selling video game console in history. It launched over 4,000 titles during its lifespan. Source: Wikipedia/PlayStation_2

facts.quizQuestion: With 155 million units shipped, which 2017 Nintendo console is the second-best-selling video game console ever?
facts.explanation: The Nintendo Switch, released in 2017, has shipped over 155 million units, making it the second-best-selling video game console of all time behind only the PlayStation 2. Its hybrid home/portable design was a major innovation. Source: Wikipedia/Nintendo_Switch

facts.quizQuestion: Which bundled Nintendo game with motion controls sold 82.90 million copies, making it the third-best-selling game ever?
facts.explanation: Wii Sports (2006), bundled with the Nintendo Wii, sold 82.90 million copies and is the third-best-selling video game of all time. Its motion controls revolutionized casual gaming and helped the Wii reach a broader audience than any prior Nintendo console. Source: Wikipedia/Wii_Sports
distractors: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess

facts.quizQuestion: Which 1989 Nintendo handheld, bundled with Tetris, sold 118.69 million units including the Color variant?
facts.explanation: The Game Boy (1989) sold 118.69 million units combined with the Game Boy Color. Its bundling with Tetris was a key sales driver, and it dominated the handheld market for a decade. Source: Wikipedia/Game_Boy

facts.quizQuestion: The Nintendo 64 controller (1996) was the first game controller to include a standard what, enabling true 3D movement in games?
facts.explanation: The Nintendo 64 (1996) controller introduced the first standard analog stick on a mainstream console controller, enabling fluid 3D movement that became foundational for modern gaming. Time magazine named it 'Machine of the Year.' Source: Wikipedia/Nintendo_64

facts.quizQuestion: At its 2010 peak, how many million subscribers did World of Warcraft have?
facts.explanation: World of Warcraft peaked at 12 million subscribers in 2010, making it the dominant massively multiplayer online game. It has generated over $9.23 billion in revenue since launching in 2004. Source: Wikipedia/World_of_Warcraft

facts.quizQuestion: Which studio, known for real-time strategy games, released World of Warcraft in 2004?
facts.explanation: Blizzard Entertainment released World of Warcraft in 2004. Having previously made StarCraft and Warcraft III, Blizzard made the leap to MMORPGs with massive success, attracting 12 million subscribers at its 2010 peak. Source: Wikipedia/World_of_Warcraft

facts.quizQuestion: Who created the sandbox game Minecraft, released through Mojang in 2011?
facts.explanation: Markus 'Notch' Persson created Minecraft, officially released in 2011 through his studio Mojang. It has sold over 350 million copies, making it the best-selling video game in history. Microsoft acquired Mojang for $2.5 billion in 2014. Source: Wikipedia/Minecraft

facts.quizQuestion: How many million copies has Minecraft sold, making it the best-selling video game in history?
facts.explanation: Minecraft has sold over 350 million copies across all platforms, making it the best-selling individual video game ever. Creator Markus 'Notch' Persson sold Mojang to Microsoft in 2014 for $2.5 billion. Source: Wikipedia/Minecraft

facts.quizQuestion: How many million concurrent viewers watched the 2019 League of Legends World Championship, demonstrating esports' mainstream scale?
facts.explanation: The 2019 League of Legends World Championship drew over 100 million concurrent viewers, rivaling traditional sports broadcasts. Riot Games' League of Legends generated $150 million per month in revenue in 2016. Source: Wikipedia/League_of_Legends

facts.quizQuestion: How many million players did Fortnite attract in its first year after launching in 2017?
facts.explanation: Fortnite attracted over 125 million players in its first year after launching in 2017, and generated over $9 billion in revenue through 2019. A Ninja and Drake livestream became the most-watched gaming stream at the time. Source: Wikipedia/Fortnite

facts.quizQuestion: Which American studio, also known for the Unreal Engine, developed Fortnite?
facts.explanation: Fortnite was developed by Epic Games, which is also famous for creating the Unreal Engine — used by countless other developers. Fortnite's battle royale mode launched in 2017 and rapidly became a cultural phenomenon. Source: Wikipedia/Fortnite

facts.quizQuestion: Which 2018 social deduction game by Innersloth became a COVID-19 pandemic phenomenon with 500 million players by November 2020?
facts.explanation: Among Us was released in 2018 but went largely unnoticed until the COVID-19 pandemic drove 500 million players to the game by November 2020. The game's social deduction mechanics — finding impostors among crewmates — made it perfect for online play. Source: Wikipedia/Among_Us

facts.quizQuestion: How many million downloads did Pokémon GO achieve in its first 33 days after launching in 2016?
facts.explanation: Pokémon GO reached 100 million downloads in just 33 days after its July 2016 launch, setting a record at the time. The augmented reality game pioneered mass-market AR gaming and generated over $6 billion in revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon_Go

facts.quizQuestion: Which augmented reality company, spun out of Google, developed Pokémon GO?
facts.explanation: Niantic, a company spun out of Google in 2015, developed Pokémon GO in partnership with The Pokémon Company. The game pioneered mass-market AR gaming and generated over $6 billion in revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon_Go

facts.quizQuestion: From how many publications did Elden Ring (2022) win Game of the Year awards?
facts.explanation: Elden Ring, released in 2022 by FromSoftware and directed by Hidetaka Miyazaki, won Game of the Year from over 300 publications. George R.R. Martin contributed to the game's worldbuilding. It sold 30 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Elden_Ring

facts.quizQuestion: Which author of A Song of Ice and Fire contributed worldbuilding lore to Elden Ring?
facts.explanation: George R.R. Martin, best known for Game of Thrones, collaborated with FromSoftware director Hidetaka Miyazaki on Elden Ring's world lore. The game won over 300 Game of the Year awards and sold 30 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Elden_Ring

facts.quizQuestion: Which Belgian studio developed Baldur's Gate 3, the first game to win Game of the Year at all 5 major gaming ceremonies?
facts.explanation: Larian Studios, based in Belgium, developed Baldur's Gate 3 (2023). It became the first game ever to win Game of the Year at all five major gaming ceremonies, including The Game Awards, BAFTA, GDC, DICE, and IGF. It sold over 20 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Baldur%27s_Gate_3

facts.quizQuestion: Grand Theft Auto V earned $1 billion in how many days after launch — setting a record as the fastest entertainment product to do so?
facts.explanation: Grand Theft Auto V (2013) earned $1 billion in just 3 days, making it the fastest entertainment product ever to reach that milestone — surpassing even major film releases. It has since sold 225 million copies. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto_V

facts.quizQuestion: How many million copies has Grand Theft Auto V sold since its 2013 release?
facts.explanation: Grand Theft Auto V has sold approximately 225 million copies since its 2013 release, making it one of the best-selling video games in history. It generated $1 billion in its first 3 days alone. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto_V

facts.quizQuestion: Which Sony-owned studio developed The Last of Us (2013), directed by Neil Druckmann?
facts.explanation: Naughty Dog, a Sony-owned studio known for the Uncharted series, developed The Last of Us (2013) under director Neil Druckmann. The game earned a 95 Metacritic score and was later adapted into a hit HBO series. Source: Wikipedia/The_Last_of_Us

facts.quizQuestion: Which developer created Guitar Hero in 2005, sparking the rhythm game boom?
facts.explanation: Harmonix developed the original Guitar Hero (2005), which sparked the rhythm game boom of the mid-2000s and grew into a $1 billion franchise. Harmonix later developed Rock Band. Source: Wikipedia/Guitar_Hero

facts.quizQuestion: Which Finnish studio, facing bankruptcy before its hit game, developed Angry Birds in 2009?
facts.explanation: Rovio, a Finnish mobile game developer, created Angry Birds in 2009 after a string of failures nearly drove the company into bankruptcy. The game reached 50 million downloads and spawned films, theme parks, and merchandise. Source: Wikipedia/Angry_Birds

facts.quizQuestion: By what percentage did North American video game revenue decline during the crash of 1983, falling from $3.2 billion to roughly $100 million?
facts.explanation: The video game crash of 1983 was catastrophic: North American revenue collapsed from $3.2 billion to just $100 million — a 97% decline. The Nintendo Entertainment System, released in 1985, rescued the market and restored consumer trust. Source: Wikipedia/Video_game_crash_of_1983

facts.quizQuestion: Which console, released in North America in 1985, rescued the video game industry after the catastrophic 1983 market crash?
facts.explanation: After the 1983 video game crash wiped out 97% of North American revenue, the Nintendo Entertainment System (released in North America in 1985) rescued the industry. Nintendo's strict quality licensing system prevented the market flooding that caused the crash. Source: Wikipedia/Video_game_crash_of_1983

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Spider-Man make his comic book debut in Amazing Fantasy #15?
facts.explanation: Spider-Man first appeared in Amazing Fantasy #15, published in 1962. Created by Stan Lee and Steve Ditko, the teenage hero with relatable personal problems became an instant hit with young readers. Source: Wikipedia/Spider-Man

facts.quizQuestion: Which artist co-created Spider-Man alongside writer Stan Lee?
facts.explanation: Steve Ditko co-created Spider-Man with Stan Lee, designing the character's costume and contributing key plot concepts. Ditko and Lee together crafted a teenage superhero whose personal struggles resonated deeply with young readers. Source: Wikipedia/Spider-Man

facts.quizQuestion: The first issue of The X-Men was published in what year?
facts.explanation: The X-Men debuted in The X-Men #1 in 1963, created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. Unlike other heroes, the mutant team was born with their powers, eliminating the need for separate origin stories for each member. Source: Wikipedia/X-Men

facts.quizQuestion: Stan Lee co-created the X-Men with which legendary Marvel artist?
facts.explanation: Stan Lee and Jack Kirby co-created the X-Men in 1963. The concept of mutants born with superhuman abilities — rather than acquiring powers through accidents — was an innovative storytelling device that defined the franchise for decades. Source: Wikipedia/X-Men

facts.quizQuestion: Iron Man, Thor, Hulk, and Ant-Man were four of the five original Avengers — who was the fifth founding member?
facts.explanation: The Wasp (Janet Van Dyne) was the fifth founding Avenger alongside Iron Man, Ant-Man, Thor, and Hulk when The Avengers #1 debuted in 1963. She is often overlooked but was present from the very beginning. Source: Wikipedia/Avengers

facts.quizQuestion: The Avengers were created in 1963 partly as Marvel's response to which rival DC superhero team?
facts.explanation: Stan Lee and Jack Kirby created the Avengers in 1963 largely as a response to DC's Justice League of America. Marvel publisher Martin Goodman reportedly instructed Lee to create a team book after hearing about JLA's strong sales. Source: Wikipedia/Avengers

facts.quizQuestion: In which 1966 comic book issue did Black Panther make his debut as the first Black superhero in American mainstream comics?
facts.explanation: Black Panther first appeared in Fantastic Four #52 in 1966, created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. Notably, this debut predated the formation of the Black Panther Party political organization, despite the shared name. Source: Wikipedia/Black Panther

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Black Panther debut as the first Black superhero in American mainstream comics?
facts.explanation: Black Panther debuted in Fantastic Four #52 in 1966, created by Stan Lee and Jack Kirby. His arrival predated the Black Panther Party political organization, making the name coincidence a source of frequent historical confusion. Source: Wikipedia/Black Panther

facts.quizQuestion: Jack Kirby co-created Captain America with which other writer-artist in 1941?
facts.explanation: Joe Simon and Jack Kirby co-created Captain America Comics #1 in 1941. The cover famously depicted Captain America punching Adolf Hitler — a bold statement published months before the United States entered World War II. Source: Wikipedia/Captain America

facts.quizQuestion: Captain America Comics #1 — which showed the hero punching Hitler on its cover — was published in what year?
facts.explanation: Captain America Comics #1 debuted in 1941, created by Joe Simon and Jack Kirby. The issue sold approximately one million copies and appeared months before the United States officially entered World War II after Pearl Harbor. Source: Wikipedia/Captain America

facts.quizQuestion: In which year did Superman first appear in Action Comics #1, establishing the superhero genre?
facts.explanation: Superman debuted in Action Comics #1 in 1938, created by Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster. The character effectively invented the superhero genre, and the creators infamously sold the copyright for just $130. Source: Wikipedia/Superman

facts.quizQuestion: Joe Shuster drew Superman, but who wrote the character and co-created him?
facts.explanation: Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster co-created Superman, who debuted in Action Comics #1 in 1938. The pair sold the copyright for $130 — a decision that became one of the most debated creator rights stories in comic book history. Source: Wikipedia/Superman

facts.quizQuestion: In which comic book issue did Batman make his first appearance in 1939?
facts.explanation: Batman first appeared in Detective Comics #27 in 1939, created by Bob Kane with crucial contributions from Bill Finger. The 'DC' in DC Comics actually stands for 'Detective Comics,' the anthology series where Batman debuted. Source: Wikipedia/Batman

facts.quizQuestion: Despite designing Batman's iconic cowl, cape, and gloves and writing many early stories, Bill Finger was not officially credited as a co-creator until what year?
facts.explanation: Bill Finger received official co-creator credit from DC Comics/Warner Bros. in 2015, more than 75 years after Batman's debut. Finger designed the costume and wrote key stories, while Bob Kane originally received sole credit. Source: Wikipedia/Batman

facts.quizQuestion: Wonder Woman was created by a psychologist who wanted her to embody triumph through love — what was his name?
facts.explanation: William Moulton Marston, a psychologist who also helped develop the lie detector test, created Wonder Woman in 1941. He deliberately designed her to triumph through love and compassion rather than violence, making her thematically distinct from male heroes. Source: Wikipedia/Wonder Woman

facts.quizQuestion: Wonder Woman made her debut in All Star Comics #8 in what year?
facts.explanation: Wonder Woman first appeared in All Star Comics #8 in 1941, created by psychologist William Moulton Marston. She was designed as a feminist icon who would defeat enemies through love rather than brute force. Source: Wikipedia/Wonder Woman

facts.quizQuestion: Dave Gibbons drew Watchmen — which British writer authored the groundbreaking 12-issue limited series?
facts.explanation: Alan Moore wrote Watchmen, illustrated by Dave Gibbons, published from 1986 to 1987. The series fundamentally deconstructed the superhero genre and was later included in Time magazine's list of the 100 best English-language novels. Source: Wikipedia/Watchmen

facts.quizQuestion: Watchmen by Alan Moore and Dave Gibbons helped popularize what term for a self-contained book-length comic narrative?
facts.explanation: Watchmen helped mainstream the term 'graphic novel' for an extended, self-contained comic narrative intended as serious literature. Originally published as a 12-issue limited series from 1986-1987, it became one of the defining examples of the format. Source: Wikipedia/Watchmen

facts.quizQuestion: Robert Kirkman's The Walking Dead was published by which independent comics company known for creator-owned titles?
facts.explanation: The Walking Dead was published by Image Comics starting in 2003, created by Robert Kirkman and artist Tony Moore. Image Comics was founded in 1992 specifically to allow artists and writers to retain ownership of their work. Source: Wikipedia/The Walking Dead

facts.quizQuestion: How many total issues did Robert Kirkman's The Walking Dead comic series run before its surprise 2019 ending?
facts.explanation: The Walking Dead ran for 193 issues before Robert Kirkman ended it without warning in July 2019. Kirkman stated he ended the series abruptly to preserve its integrity and avoid a slow decline — the final issue shipped with no advance announcement. Source: Wikipedia/The Walking Dead

facts.quizQuestion: Which former Spider-Man artist created Spawn for Image Comics in 1992?
facts.explanation: Todd McFarlane created Spawn in 1992, published by Image Comics. Issue #1 sold 1.7 million copies, setting a record for an independent comic book. McFarlane had previously become famous for his acclaimed run as artist on The Amazing Spider-Man. Source: Wikipedia/Spawn

facts.quizQuestion: Spawn #1 set a record for independent comics by selling approximately how many million copies?
facts.explanation: Spawn #1 sold approximately 1.7 million copies when it debuted in 1992, setting a record for an independently published comic book. Creator Todd McFarlane had been one of Marvel's top-selling Spider-Man artists before leaving to found Image Comics. Source: Wikipedia/Spawn

facts.quizQuestion: Peter Laird co-created the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles with which artist partner in 1984?
facts.explanation: Kevin Eastman and Peter Laird created the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles in 1984 for Mirage Studios. Originally conceived as a parody of Frank Miller's Daredevil and Chris Claremont's X-Men, the first print run was just 3,275 copies. Source: Wikipedia/TMNT

facts.quizQuestion: The first issue of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles had an initial print run of only how many copies?
facts.explanation: The first Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles issue had an initial print run of just 3,275 copies in 1984. Despite these humble origins as a parody, the franchise exploded into one of the most successful entertainment properties of the 1980s and 1990s. Source: Wikipedia/TMNT

facts.quizQuestion: Senate hearings on comics' impact on youth led to the formation of the Comics Code Authority in what year?
facts.explanation: The Comics Code Authority was established in 1954 following Senate subcommittee hearings that examined whether comic books contributed to juvenile delinquency. It imposed 41 content restrictions and effectively drove EC Comics — known for horror titles — out of business. Source: Wikipedia/Comics Code Authority

facts.quizQuestion: When founded in 1954, how many content restrictions did the Comics Code Authority impose on publishers?
facts.explanation: The Comics Code Authority imposed 41 content restrictions covering violence, sexual content, and depictions of authority figures when it was established in 1954. It remained in force until 2011, when the last publishers abandoned it. Source: Wikipedia/Comics Code Authority

facts.quizQuestion: Stan Lee co-created Spider-Man, X-Men, Iron Man, Thor, and Hulk — which superhero team also belongs to his creations?
facts.explanation: Stan Lee co-created the Fantastic Four alongside Jack Kirby, along with Spider-Man, X-Men, Iron Man, Thor, and the Hulk. Lee received the National Medal of Arts and became famous for cameo appearances in Marvel films. Source: Wikipedia/Stan Lee

facts.quizQuestion: Stan Lee, who passed away in 2018, was born in what year?
facts.explanation: Stan Lee was born in 1922 and died in 2018 at the age of 95. He spent his career at Marvel Comics, co-creating some of the most enduring superhero characters in pop culture history and serving as Marvel's creative leader for decades. Source: Wikipedia/Stan Lee

facts.quizQuestion: Jack Kirby co-created Captain America, the Fantastic Four, the X-Men, and Black Panther — what celebrated nickname did he earn?
facts.explanation: Jack Kirby earned the nickname 'King of Comics' for his unparalleled influence on the medium. Born in 1917, he co-created some of Marvel's most iconic characters alongside Stan Lee and Joe Simon, shaping the visual language of superhero comics for generations. Source: Wikipedia/Jack Kirby

facts.quizQuestion: Jack Kirby, who died in 1994, was born in what year?
facts.explanation: Jack Kirby was born in 1917 and died in 1994 at the age of 76. Nicknamed the 'King of Comics,' he co-created Captain America in 1941 and continued to define superhero comics for more than 50 years. Source: Wikipedia/Jack Kirby

facts.quizQuestion: On which website did the Rickroll meme originate in May 2007?
facts.explanation: The Rickroll originated on 4chan when user Shawn Cotter posted a link claiming to be a Grand Theft Auto IV trailer that instead played Rick Astley's 'Never Gonna Give You Up.' The bait-and-switch trick is still active 18+ years later. Source: Wikipedia/Rickrolling

facts.quizQuestion: What was the username of the 4chan user credited with starting the Rickroll meme in 2007?
facts.explanation: The Rickroll was started on 4chan by a user named Shawn Cotter, who posted a deceptive link claiming to lead to a Grand Theft Auto IV trailer. The prank spread worldwide and remains a beloved internet tradition. Source: Wikipedia/Rickrolling

facts.quizQuestion: Who created the Nyan Cat animation in April 2011?
facts.explanation: Christopher Torres created Nyan Cat in April 2011 — a pixelated cat with a Pop-Tart body flying through space with a rainbow trail. It became the 5th most viewed YouTube video of 2011. Source: Wikipedia/Nyan Cat

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Nyan Cat become the 5th most viewed YouTube video?
facts.explanation: Nyan Cat was created and went viral in April 2011, finishing the year as the 5th most-viewed video on YouTube. The looping Pop-Tart cat with a rainbow trail and catchy soundtrack captivated millions worldwide. Source: Wikipedia/Nyan Cat

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was the original Keyboard Cat footage recorded on VHS?
facts.explanation: The original Keyboard Cat video was recorded on VHS in 1984, featuring a cat named Fatso being held upright and 'playing' a keyboard. The footage was uploaded to YouTube in 2007 and went viral in 2009, long after the cat had already died in 1987. Source: Wikipedia/Keyboard Cat

facts.quizQuestion: What was the name of the cat in the original Keyboard Cat footage filmed in 1984?
facts.explanation: The original Keyboard Cat was a cat named Fatso, owned by Charlie Schmidt. The footage was shot on VHS in 1984, but Fatso died in 1987 — making the beloved internet cat famous more than 20 years after its death. Source: Wikipedia/Keyboard Cat

facts.quizQuestion: What was the name of the Shiba Inu whose photo became the Doge meme?
facts.explanation: The Doge meme features a Shiba Inu named Kabosu, whose photo went viral in February 2010. Kabosu's image became one of the internet's most recognizable memes, later inspiring Dogecoin in December 2013. Kabosu passed away in May 2024. Source: Wikipedia/Doge meme

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was Dogecoin launched, inspired by the Doge meme?
facts.explanation: Dogecoin was launched in December 2013, inspired by the Doge Shiba Inu meme that had gone viral in 2010. The cryptocurrency was initially created as a joke but eventually reached a market cap of over $80 billion at its peak in 2021. Source: Wikipedia/Doge meme

facts.quizQuestion: Which artist created Pepe the Frog in his 2005 webcomic?
facts.explanation: Matt Furie created Pepe the Frog in his 2005 webcomic 'Boy's Club.' The 'Feels good man' frog went viral on 4chan from 2008 onward. Furie has spent years trying to reclaim Pepe as a symbol of positivity. Source: Wikipedia/Pepe the Frog

facts.quizQuestion: What was the name of Matt Furie's webcomic where Pepe the Frog first appeared in 2005?
facts.explanation: Pepe the Frog debuted in Matt Furie's 2005 webcomic 'Boy's Club,' originally as a laid-back character whose catchphrase was 'Feels good man.' The character was later widely appropriated as a meme, beginning around 2008. Source: Wikipedia/Pepe the Frog

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million views did 'Charlie Bit My Finger' accumulate on YouTube?
facts.explanation: 'Charlie Bit My Finger,' uploaded in May 2007, amassed 888+ million views, making it the #1 ranked video in TIME's list of YouTube's 50 greatest viral videos. The original was later removed and sold as an NFT for $760,999 in 2021. Source: Wikipedia/Charlie Bit My Finger

facts.quizQuestion: For how many dollars was the 'Charlie Bit My Finger' video sold as an NFT in 2021?
facts.explanation: 'Charlie Bit My Finger' was sold as an NFT for $760,999 in 2021, after which the original was removed from YouTube. The video, uploaded in May 2007, had accumulated 888 million views and was considered a landmark of early internet culture. Source: Wikipedia/Charlie Bit My Finger

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Gangnam Style become the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views?
facts.explanation: Gangnam Style by PSY, released in July 2012, became the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views in December 2012. It was a turning point for K-pop's global visibility and now has over 5 billion views. Source: Wikipedia/Gangnam Style

facts.quizQuestion: Which South Korean rapper performed the globally viral Gangnam Style music video in 2012?
facts.explanation: PSY (Park Jae-sang) released Gangnam Style in July 2012. The song and its accompanying horse-riding dance became a global phenomenon and a turning point for K-pop's international reach. Source: Wikipedia/Gangnam Style

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Baby Shark become the first YouTube video to reach 10 billion views?
facts.explanation: Baby Shark by Pinkfong reached 10 billion views in January 2022, making it the most-viewed video in YouTube history. Originally uploaded in June 2016, it now has over 16 billion views and holds the Guinness World Record. Source: Wikipedia/Baby Shark

facts.quizQuestion: Which South Korean entertainment company uploaded Baby Shark to YouTube in 2016?
facts.explanation: Baby Shark was uploaded by Pinkfong, a South Korean education and entertainment brand, in June 2016. The video became the most-viewed video in YouTube history, surpassing Gangnam Style to reach 10 billion views in January 2022. Source: Wikipedia/Baby Shark

facts.quizQuestion: On which platform did Gary Brolsma originally post his Numa Numa lip-sync video in December 2004?
facts.explanation: Gary Brolsma posted his lip-sync to 'Dragostea Din Tei' by O-Zone on Newgrounds in December 2004. The video accumulated hundreds of millions of views and is considered one of the first great viral internet videos. Source: Wikipedia/Numa Numa

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Mark Zuckerberg launch Facebook from his Harvard dorm room?
facts.explanation: Mark Zuckerberg launched Facebook on February 4, 2004, from his Harvard dormitory. The site reached 1 million users by the end of 2004 and received its first major investment — $500,000 from Peter Thiel — that same year. Source: Wikipedia/Facebook

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Jack Dorsey send the first tweet, 'just setting up my twttr'?
facts.explanation: Jack Dorsey sent the first tweet — 'just setting up my twttr' — on March 21, 2006. Twitter then launched publicly on July 15, 2006. The platform became a global venue for breaking news and cultural commentary. Source: Wikipedia/Twitter

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did ByteDance launch TikTok for international audiences outside China?
facts.explanation: ByteDance launched TikTok globally in September 2017, separate from its Chinese version Douyin (launched September 2016). ByteDance then acquired Musical.ly for approximately $1 billion in November 2017 and merged it into TikTok in 2018. Source: Wikipedia/TikTok

facts.quizQuestion: Who co-founded Instagram alongside Mike Krieger in October 2010?
facts.explanation: Kevin Systrom co-founded Instagram with Mike Krieger, launching the app on October 6, 2010. Instagram gained 25,000 users on its first day, reached 1 million users in under three months, and was acquired by Facebook for $1 billion in 2012. Source: Wikipedia/Instagram

facts.quizQuestion: For how many billion dollars did Facebook acquire Instagram in 2012?
facts.explanation: Facebook acquired Instagram for $1 billion in 2012, just 18 months after the app launched. At the time, Instagram had about 30 million users and no revenue — the price shocked the tech world. Source: Wikipedia/Instagram

facts.quizQuestion: Who uploaded 'Me at the zoo,' the first video ever posted on YouTube?
facts.explanation: Jawed Karim, a YouTube co-founder, uploaded 'Me at the zoo' on April 23, 2005. The 19-second clip filmed at the San Diego Zoo has over 385 million views and is preserved as a landmark of internet history. Source: Wikipedia/Me at the zoo

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was 'Me at the zoo,' the first YouTube video, uploaded?
facts.explanation: Jawed Karim uploaded 'Me at the zoo' on April 23, 2005, marking the beginning of YouTube's public history. The 19-second clip was filmed at the San Diego Zoo and has since accumulated over 385 million views. Source: Wikipedia/Me at the zoo

facts.quizQuestion: Who co-founded Wikipedia alongside Larry Sanger in January 2001?
facts.explanation: Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger co-founded Wikipedia on January 15, 2001. It was preceded by Nupedia, a more traditional expert-reviewed encyclopedia launched in March 2000. Wikipedia grew to become the largest reference site in human history. Source: Wikipedia/Wikipedia

facts.quizQuestion: Who co-founded Reddit alongside Alexis Ohanian in June 2005?
facts.explanation: Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian co-founded Reddit in June 2005. The site was sold to Condé Nast in October 2006 for an estimated $10-20 million. Reddit became one of the most visited websites in the world, known as 'the front page of the internet.' Source: Wikipedia/Reddit

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Jason Citron and Stanislav Vishnevskiy launch Discord?
facts.explanation: Jason Citron and Stanislav Vishnevskiy launched Discord in May 2015, originally designed for gamers to communicate during play sessions. Discord grew far beyond gaming to become a major platform for communities of all kinds. Source: Wikipedia/Discord

facts.quizQuestion: What were the actual colors of the dress at the center of the viral 2015 'The Dress' debate?
facts.explanation: The famous dress from the February 25, 2015 viral debate was actually blue and black, though millions perceived it as white and gold due to differences in color perception and ambient light interpretation. The debate generated over 2.2 million tweets in under 24 hours. Source: Wikipedia/The dress

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many million dollars did the Ice Bucket Challenge raise for ALS research in 2014?
facts.explanation: The Ice Bucket Challenge, popularized in July-August 2014 by Pete Frates and Pat Quinn, raised over $115 million for ALS (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) research. The challenge generated 1.2 million Facebook videos and became a landmark of social media fundraising. Source: Wikipedia/Ice Bucket Challenge

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did The New York Times acquire the word game Wordle from its creator?
facts.explanation: The New York Times acquired Wordle from creator Josh Wardle in January 2022 for a 'seven-figure sum.' Wordle had grown from 90 players when it launched publicly in October 2021 to over 2 million players in just two months. Source: Wikipedia/Wordle

facts.quizQuestion: Which Vietnamese developer created Flappy Bird before removing it from app stores in February 2014?
facts.explanation: Dong Nguyen created Flappy Bird in May 2013 under his studio .GEARS. The game reached 50 million downloads and generated $50,000 per day in ad revenue before Nguyen famously removed it from app stores in February 2014, saying it was 'too addictive.' Source: Wikipedia/Flappy Bird

facts.quizQuestion: Which media franchise is the highest-grossing of all time with over $147 billion in total revenue?
facts.explanation: Pokémon has generated over $147 billion since its creation in 1996, surpassing Hello Kitty, Star Wars, and every other media franchise through video games, trading cards, merchandise, anime, and more. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

facts.quizQuestion: Who created Pokémon in 1996, partly inspired by his childhood hobby of collecting insects?
facts.explanation: Satoshi Tajiri, along with Ken Sugimori, created Pokémon for Game Freak / Nintendo. Tajiri's childhood love of insect collecting in the countryside directly inspired the concept of catching and collecting creatures. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was the Pokémon franchise first launched?
facts.explanation: Pokémon debuted in 1996 with the release of Pocket Monsters Red and Green for the Game Boy in Japan. The franchise has since grown to include anime, trading cards, movies, merchandise, and apps like Pokémon GO. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

facts.quizQuestion: Which designer created the iconic Hello Kitty character for Sanrio in 1974?
facts.explanation: Yuko Shimizu designed Hello Kitty for the Japanese company Sanrio in 1974. The character has since become one of the most recognizable brand icons globally, appearing on over 50,000 products in more than 130 countries. Source: Wikipedia/Hello_Kitty

facts.quizQuestion: Which Japanese company owns the Hello Kitty brand and produces over 50,000 products worldwide?
facts.explanation: Sanrio is the Japanese company that owns Hello Kitty, created in 1974. The brand generates over $80 billion in total revenue and operates in more than 130 countries with tens of thousands of licensed products. Source: Wikipedia/Hello_Kitty

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many different products feature the Hello Kitty brand globally?
facts.explanation: Hello Kitty appears on over 50,000 products sold in more than 130 countries, making it one of the most extensively merchandised characters in history. The character generates over $80 billion in total revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Hello_Kitty

facts.quizQuestion: Which filmmaker created the Star Wars universe, first releasing it to cinemas in 1977?
facts.explanation: George Lucas created Star Wars, which debuted in 1977 and became one of the most financially successful franchises in history, generating over $65 billion. Lucas later sold the franchise to The Walt Disney Company in 2012. Source: Wikipedia/Star_Wars

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did the original Star Wars film first hit theaters, launching one of the biggest franchises in history?
facts.explanation: Star Wars: Episode IV — A New Hope was released in 1977, created by George Lucas. It launched a franchise that has since generated over $65 billion across films, merchandise, theme parks, and expanded universe media. Source: Wikipedia/Star_Wars

facts.quizQuestion: Which British author created the Harry Potter series, first publishing the debut novel in 1997?
facts.explanation: J.K. Rowling created the Harry Potter series, beginning with Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone in 1997. The franchise has sold over 500 million books and generated $25–43 billion across books, films, merchandise, and theme parks. Source: Wikipedia/Harry_Potter

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many millions of copies of Harry Potter books have been sold worldwide?
facts.explanation: The Harry Potter book series has sold over 500 million copies worldwide, making it one of the best-selling book series in history. Combined with 8 films grossing $8.5 billion, the franchise total reaches $25–43 billion. Source: Wikipedia/Harry_Potter

facts.quizQuestion: How many billion dollars did the 8 mainline Harry Potter films gross at the global box office?
facts.explanation: The 8 Harry Potter films grossed approximately $8.5 billion at the worldwide box office, making it one of the most successful film series of all time. The wider franchise including books, merchandise, and theme parks generates $25–43 billion in total. Source: Wikipedia/Harry_Potter

facts.quizQuestion: Which 2008 superhero film launched the Marvel Cinematic Universe franchise?
facts.explanation: Iron Man (2008), directed by Jon Favreau and starring Robert Downey Jr., was the first film in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. The MCU has since become the highest-grossing film franchise of all time with over $29 billion at the global box office. Source: Wikipedia/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did the Marvel Cinematic Universe officially begin with the release of Iron Man?
facts.explanation: The Marvel Cinematic Universe launched in 2008 with Iron Man. Over the following 15+ years, the MCU expanded to 30+ films, becoming the highest-grossing film franchise of all time with over $29 billion in worldwide box office revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe

facts.quizQuestion: Which film franchise holds the record as the highest-grossing of all time with over $29 billion at the box office?
facts.explanation: The Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU) is the highest-grossing film franchise ever, surpassing the James Bond, Star Wars, and Harry Potter series. It launched in 2008 with Iron Man and has produced 30+ films. Source: Wikipedia/Marvel_Cinematic_Universe

facts.quizQuestion: Which Nintendo designer created Mario, originally because the company couldn't license the Popeye character?
facts.explanation: Shigeru Miyamoto created Mario after Nintendo failed to acquire the Popeye license for an arcade game. The Mario franchise has since become the best-selling video game franchise of all time, with hundreds of millions of games sold. Source: Wikipedia/Mario

facts.quizQuestion: Which Nintendo franchise holds the title of the best-selling video game series of all time?
facts.explanation: The Mario franchise is the best-selling video game franchise in history. Originally created by Shigeru Miyamoto after Nintendo couldn't license Popeye, Mario has appeared in hundreds of games across every Nintendo platform since the 1980s. Source: Wikipedia/Mario

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did the Call of Duty franchise launch its first game?
facts.explanation: Call of Duty debuted in 2003, developed by Infinity Ward and published by Activision. The franchise has since sold over 500 million copies and generated over $30 billion in lifetime revenue, making it one of gaming's most commercially successful series. Source: Wikipedia/Call_of_Duty

facts.quizQuestion: Which game studio originally developed the first Call of Duty game in 2003?
facts.explanation: Infinity Ward developed the original Call of Duty in 2003 in partnership with Activision. The franchise has grown to include multiple studios and has sold over 500 million copies, generating more than $30 billion in lifetime revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Call_of_Duty

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many millions of copies has the Call of Duty franchise sold across all its titles?
facts.explanation: Call of Duty has sold over 500 million copies since its 2003 debut, generating more than $30 billion in lifetime revenue. It consistently ranks as one of the top-selling game franchises alongside Mario and Pokémon. Source: Wikipedia/Call_of_Duty

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did the Grand Theft Auto franchise begin with its first game?
facts.explanation: Grand Theft Auto debuted in 1997, developed by Rockstar Games. The franchise has since sold over 460 million copies, with GTA V alone generating over $10.22 billion, making it one of the highest-grossing entertainment products in history. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto

facts.quizQuestion: Which game studio developed the Grand Theft Auto franchise?
facts.explanation: Rockstar Games developed the Grand Theft Auto franchise, which debuted in 1997. The series has sold over 460 million copies and GTA V alone generated over $10.22 billion, making it one of the most profitable entertainment franchises ever. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many millions of copies has the entire Grand Theft Auto franchise sold?
facts.explanation: The Grand Theft Auto franchise has sold over 460 million copies. GTA V alone generated $10.22 billion+ since its 2013 release, making it the highest-grossing entertainment product of its time. Source: Wikipedia/Grand_Theft_Auto

facts.quizQuestion: Who founded the LEGO company in Denmark in 1932?
facts.explanation: Ole Kirk Christiansen founded the LEGO company in Billund, Denmark in 1932. The name LEGO comes from the Danish phrase 'leg godt,' meaning 'play well.' The company has produced over 6 billion bricks since 1968. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was the LEGO company founded in Denmark?
facts.explanation: LEGO was founded in 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen in Billund, Denmark. The name comes from the Danish 'leg godt' meaning 'play well.' The company has become one of the world's most recognized toy brands, producing over 6 billion bricks since 1968. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

facts.quizQuestion: The name LEGO comes from which Danish phrase meaning 'play well'?
facts.explanation: LEGO is derived from the Danish phrase 'leg godt,' meaning 'play well.' Founded in 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen in Denmark, the LEGO company has since become one of the world's most iconic toy brands, producing over 6 billion bricks since 1968. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many billion LEGO bricks have been produced since the company began making them in 1968?
facts.explanation: LEGO has produced over 6 billion bricks since 1968, when the iconic interlocking plastic brick design was standardized. The company is based in Billund, Denmark, and was founded in 1932 by Ole Kirk Christiansen. Source: Wikipedia/Lego

facts.quizQuestion: Which co-founder of Mattel created Hot Wheels in 1968?
facts.explanation: Elliot Handler, co-founder of Mattel, created Hot Wheels in 1968. The brand has since manufactured over 6 billion die-cast cars and generated $1.43 billion in revenue in 2023 alone, making it the world's best-selling toy vehicle brand. Source: Wikipedia/Hot_Wheels

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Hot Wheels launch, creating the world's best-selling toy vehicle brand?
facts.explanation: Hot Wheels launched in 1968, created by Elliot Handler of Mattel. The brand has since produced over 6 billion die-cast cars and generates over $1 billion in annual revenue, making it the dominant name in toy vehicles globally. Source: Wikipedia/Hot_Wheels

facts.quizQuestion: Approximately how many billion Hot Wheels cars have been manufactured since the brand launched?
facts.explanation: Hot Wheels has manufactured over 6 billion die-cast cars since its 1968 debut, making it the world's best-selling toy vehicle brand. Mattel generated $1.43 billion in Hot Wheels revenue in 2023. Source: Wikipedia/Hot_Wheels

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did Mattel launch the Barbie doll at the New York Toy Fair?
facts.explanation: Barbie debuted at the New York Toy Fair in 1959, created by Ruth Handler. The doll became one of the most iconic toys in history, generating $1.54 billion in revenue for Mattel in 2023, boosted further by the blockbuster 2023 film. Source: Wikipedia/Barbie

facts.quizQuestion: Which toy company owns and produces the Barbie doll brand?
facts.explanation: Mattel owns Barbie, which debuted in 1959 and has remained one of the world's best-selling toy lines. The brand generated $1.54 billion in 2023 revenue, receiving additional attention from the major 2023 blockbuster film. Source: Wikipedia/Barbie

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did the Transformers franchise debut with its toy line and animated series?
facts.explanation: The Transformers franchise launched in 1984, developed by Hasbro. It has since generated over $25 billion across toys, animated series, comics, and the Michael Bay film series, making it one of Hasbro's most valuable properties. Source: Wikipedia/Transformers

facts.quizQuestion: Which toy company owns the Transformers franchise that debuted in 1984?
facts.explanation: Hasbro owns Transformers, which launched in 1984. The franchise has generated over $25 billion total across toys, animated series, comics, and the popular Michael Bay film adaptations. Source: Wikipedia/Transformers

facts.quizQuestion: Which Hasbro franchise, featuring robots that disguise themselves as vehicles, has generated over $25 billion since launching in 1984?
facts.explanation: Transformers has generated over $25 billion since debuting in 1984. The franchise spans toys, animated TV shows, comics, and blockbuster films. It remains one of Hasbro's flagship properties. Source: Wikipedia/Transformers

facts.quizQuestion: Which video game development studio, working with Nintendo, created the original Pokémon games?
facts.explanation: Game Freak developed the original Pokémon games in collaboration with Nintendo. Founded by Satoshi Tajiri and Ken Sugimori in 1996, the franchise became the highest-grossing media franchise of all time with over $147 billion in total revenue. Source: Wikipedia/Pokémon

facts.quizQuestion: On which platform did MrBeast become the most-subscribed individual creator?
facts.explanation: Jimmy 'MrBeast' Donaldson became the most-subscribed individual on YouTube, known for expensive challenge videos and large-scale philanthropy. He started his channel in 2012 at age 13. Source: Wikipedia/MrBeast

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did MrBeast (Jimmy Donaldson) start his YouTube channel?
facts.explanation: MrBeast launched his YouTube channel in 2012 at age 13, spending years experimenting with content before his viral breakthrough. He grew into the most-subscribed individual creator on the platform. Source: Wikipedia/MrBeast

facts.quizQuestion: What nationality is Felix Kjellberg, better known as PewDiePie?
facts.explanation: Felix Kjellberg, known online as PewDiePie, is Swedish. He was the first individual creator to reach 100 million YouTube subscribers, achieving that milestone in August 2019. Source: Wikipedia/PewDiePie

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did PewDiePie become the first individual to reach 100 million YouTube subscribers?
facts.explanation: PewDiePie (Felix Kjellberg) crossed 100 million YouTube subscribers in August 2019, becoming the first individual creator to reach that milestone. He held the most-subscribed crown for years before being overtaken. Source: Wikipedia/PewDiePie

facts.quizQuestion: What is PewDiePie's real name?
facts.explanation: PewDiePie's real name is Felix Arvid Ulf Kjellberg. The Swedish YouTuber rose to fame primarily through Let's Play videos and became one of the most recognizable figures in online entertainment. Source: Wikipedia/PewDiePie

facts.quizQuestion: How many seasons did The Oprah Winfrey Show run before ending in 2011?
facts.explanation: The Oprah Winfrey Show ran for 25 seasons from 1986 to 2011. It became the highest-rated daytime talk show in history, and 'Oprah's Book Club' could instantly propel any selected title to bestseller status. Source: Wikipedia/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did The Oprah Winfrey Show premiere nationally?
facts.explanation: The Oprah Winfrey Show premiered nationally in 1986 and ran until 2011, totaling 25 seasons. It became the highest-rated daytime talk show in television history. Source: Wikipedia/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show

facts.quizQuestion: Which daytime talk show, running 25 seasons from 1986 to 2011, is considered the highest-rated in its genre's history?
facts.explanation: The Oprah Winfrey Show ran from 1986 to 2011 for 25 seasons and became the highest-rated daytime talk show in history. Oprah's endorsements and book club picks were so powerful they created the 'Oprah Effect.' Source: Wikipedia/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was San Diego Comic-Con founded?
facts.explanation: San Diego Comic-Con was founded in 1970, originally as a small comic book convention. It has since evolved into the world's premier pop culture event, drawing over 130,000 attendees annually. Source: Wikipedia/San_Diego_Comic-Con

facts.quizQuestion: In which California city is the world's most famous Comic-Con convention held annually?
facts.explanation: San Diego Comic-Con, founded in 1970, draws over 130,000 attendees annually and has evolved from a comic book convention into the world's premier pop culture event for films, TV, games, and collectibles. Source: Wikipedia/San_Diego_Comic-Con

facts.quizQuestion: Who coined the term 'cosplay' in 1984?
facts.explanation: Japanese reporter Nobuyuki Takahashi coined the term 'cosplay' in 1984 as a portmanteau of 'costume play' after attending the 1984 World Science Fiction Convention (Worldcon) in Los Angeles. Source: Wikipedia/Cosplay

facts.quizQuestion: In what year was the term 'cosplay' first coined?
facts.explanation: The term 'cosplay' was coined in 1984 by Japanese reporter Nobuyuki Takahashi. It is a portmanteau of 'costume play' and has grown into a global subculture with multi-billion dollar convention and merchandise economies. Source: Wikipedia/Cosplay

facts.quizQuestion: In what year did 'The Dress' viral phenomenon generate 2.2 million tweets in 24 hours over its ambiguous colors?
